To a flame-dried 100 mL three-neck round-bottom flask equipped with a magnetic stir bar, reflux condenser, and addition funnel, magnesium turnings (0.49 g, 20.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) and anhydrous THF (15 mL) were added under argon. A solution of bromobenzene (3.14 g, 20.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) in anhydrous THF (10 mL) was added dropwise via the addition funnel over 30 min, maintaining a gentle reflux. After the addition was complete, the mixture was stirred at reflux for 1 h to ensure complete formation of the Grignard reagent. The solution was then cooled to 0 °C in an ice bath, and benzaldehyde (1.06 g, 10.0 mmol, 1.0 equiv) in anhydrous THF (5 mL) was added dropwise over 15 min. The reaction was warmed to room temperature and stirred for 2 h. The reaction was quenched by careful addition of saturated NH4Cl (20 mL) at 0 °C. The mixture was extracted with diethyl ether (3 x 25 mL). The combined organic layers were washed with brine (20 mL), dried over MgSO4, filtered, and concentrated under reduced pressure. The crude product was purified by flash column chromatography (hexanes/EtOAc, 80:20) to afford diphenylmethanol as a white crystalline solid (1.66 g, 90% yield).
